title: Leucanthemum lanceolatum
intro: Leucanthemum lanceolatum, also known as the lance-leaved daisy, is a flowering plant species native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its daisy-like flowers and lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, and is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been linked to its ability to soothe digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Leucanthemum lanceolatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Leucanthemum lanceolatum is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Leucanthemum lanceolatum is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it in the order Asterales. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Leucanthemum lanceolatum is closely related to other flowering plants such as daisies and sunflowers. The genus Leucanthemum is a group of flowering plants commonly known as oxeye daisies. Leucanthemum lanceolatum is specifically a species of oxeye daisy found in certain regions.
